title: Karpathy's Obsidian RAG + Claude Code = CHEAT CODE
summary: Tutorial walkthrough of Karpathy's Obsidian-based knowledge base as a lightweight RAG alternative. Uses raw folder ingestion, Claude Code wiki generation, master index traversal, CLAUDE.md as controller. Key insight: markdown file structure + LLM context window = sufficient retrieval for solo devs without vector DBs or embeddings. Directly relevant to JASON-OS knowledge layer and our existing .research/ analysis pipeline.
creator_view: The core insight — structured markdown as a knowledge retrieval system — is exactly what our .research/ directory + /recall pipeline already does. The video validates our approach while showing a simpler Obsidian-native variant that trades our SQLite index for filesystem traversal via CLAUDE.md instructions.

candidates.name: Obsidian raw→wiki pipeline pattern
candidates.description: Raw folder staging → LLM-generated wiki → master index. Lightweight alternative to vector RAG.

candidates.name: CLAUDE.md as knowledge traversal controller
candidates.description: CLAUDE.md instructs LLM how to navigate the file structure, replacing vector retrieval with path-based lookup.

candidates.name: Obsidian Web Clipper as ingestion tool
candidates.description: Chrome extension that converts web pages to markdown files in the raw folder. Paired with Local Images Plus plugin for image preservation.

candidates.name: Scale threshold: when to move to real RAG
candidates.description: Solo dev / small team with <thousands of docs = Obsidian sufficient. Millions of docs = proper RAG. Start simple, migrate when scale demands.

candidates.name: Overcomplicating knowledge retrieval
candidates.description: Jumping to vector DBs, embeddings, graph RAG before validating that structured markdown + LLM context is sufficient for your scale.
